query: What is the cost of medical office construction in Texas?
base_value: Healthcare construction in Texas continues to accelerate heading into 2026 — but so do project costs. With demand rising for outpatient clinics, urgent care centers, and multi-tenant medical buildings, developers and healthcare groups are re-evaluating how much it really costs to build a medical facility in today's market.
answer_block:
Medical office construction in Texas costs $350–$800 per square foot in 2026, depending on facility type, finish level, and market. Surgical and imaging centers sit at the high end ($600–$900/SF); general practice and family clinics start near $350–$500/SF. Houston and Dallas carry the highest per-square-foot rates; Austin and San Antonio run slightly lower. Design-build delivery typically saves 10–15% in total project cost and compresses schedules by up to 30%.

  • General practice / family clinic: $350–$500/SF
  • Dental / orthodontic clinic: $400–$600/SF
  • Urgent care center: $500–$650/SF
  • Imaging / diagnostic center: $600–$800/SF
  • Ambulatory surgery center (ASC): $650–$900/SF
new_value_description: Answer block prepended immediately after H1, above existing intro paragraph. All original body content preserved unchanged below the answer block.
notes: Current P1 opens with narrative context ('Healthcare construction in Texas continues to accelerate...') — classic in-today's-market opener that gives AI engines no extractable answer.
